evals.prompt: I have a minimal ASP.NET Core Web API and I need to protect it with JWT bearer authentication. The IdentityServer authority is at https://identity.example.com and the API resource name is api1. Show me how to set it up securely.
evals.expected_output: Code that adds JWT bearer authentication with Authority, Audience, and ValidTypes set to ["at+jwt"]. Includes UseAuthentication and UseAuthorization middleware in the correct order. Mentions the Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.JwtBearer package.
assertions: Calls AddJwtBearer with Authority set to 'https://identity.example.com'
Sets Audience to 'api1'
Sets TokenValidationParameters.ValidTypes to include 'at+jwt' to prevent JWT confusion attacks
Includes UseAuthentication() before UseAuthorization() in the middleware pipeline
Mentions or adds the Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.JwtBearer package

evals.prompt: Our API receives reference tokens (opaque strings) from clients, not JWTs. How do I configure the API to validate these using token introspection? The API resource in IdentityServer is named 'invoicing' with secret 'invoicing_secret'. Authority is https://auth.mycompany.com.
evals.expected_output: Code that adds OAuth2 introspection with the correct authority, client ID, and client secret. Mentions the Duende package needed. Notes that the ApiResource in IdentityServer must have ApiSecrets configured.
assertions: Calls AddOAuth2Introspection with Authority set to 'https://auth.mycompany.com'
Sets ClientId to 'invoicing' and ClientSecret to 'invoicing_secret'
Mentions or adds the Duende.AspNetCore.Authentication.OAuth2Introspection or Duende.IdentityServer.AccessTokenValidation package
Warns that the ApiResource in IdentityServer must have ApiSecrets configured for introspection to work

evals.prompt: We need our API to accept both JWT access tokens and reference tokens. Some clients get JWTs, others get reference tokens. How do I handle both in a single API? Authority is https://identity.example.com, API resource is api1, secret is api1_secret.
evals.expected_output: Code that configures both AddJwtBearer and AddOAuth2Introspection, using ForwardReferenceToken from the Duende.AspNetCore.Authentication.JwtBearer package to route tokens to the correct handler based on whether they contain a dot.
assertions: Configures both AddJwtBearer and AddOAuth2Introspection handlers
Uses ForwardReferenceToken (via Selector.ForwardReferenceToken) to route reference tokens to the introspection handler
The scheme name passed to ForwardReferenceToken matches the scheme name used in AddOAuth2Introspection
Sets ValidTypes to include 'at+jwt' on the JWT bearer handler
Mentions or adds the Duende.AspNetCore.Authentication.JwtBearer package

evals.prompt: I need scope-based authorization for my API. The /data GET endpoint should require 'api1.read' scope and the /data POST endpoint should require 'api1.write' scope. Also, our IdentityServer sometimes emits scopes as a space-delimited string. How do I handle that?
evals.expected_output: Code that defines authorization policies using RequireClaim for scope, applies them to endpoints with RequireAuthorization, and includes a custom IClaimsTransformation to normalize space-delimited scope claims into individual claims.
assertions: Defines authorization policies that use RequireClaim with 'scope' claim type for 'api1.read' and 'api1.write'
Applies the read policy to the GET endpoint and the write policy to the POST endpoint using RequireAuthorization
Implements a custom IClaimsTransformation that splits space-delimited scope claims into individual claims
Registers the IClaimsTransformation implementation in the DI container

evals.prompt: I need to add DPoP (Demonstration of Proof-of-Possession) support to my API. The API uses JWT bearer auth with scheme name 'token', authority https://identity.example.com, audience api1. Show me the correct way to configure DPoP validation.
evals.expected_output: Code that calls ConfigureDPoPTokensForScheme on IServiceCollection (NOT inside AddJwtBearer lambda), registers a distributed cache for replay detection, and sets up JWT bearer authentication.
assertions: Calls ConfigureDPoPTokensForScheme on IServiceCollection (builder.Services), NOT inside the AddJwtBearer options lambda
Registers a distributed cache implementation (e.g., AddDistributedMemoryCache or Redis) for DPoP replay detection
Sets ValidTypes to include 'at+jwt' on the JWT bearer handler
Mentions or adds the Duende.AspNetCore.Authentication.JwtBearer package

evals.prompt: I'm hosting an API inside the same application as my IdentityServer. I don't want to go through network calls to validate tokens for this local API. How do I protect these local API endpoints?
evals.expected_output: Code that calls AddLocalApiAuthentication() and protects endpoints using IdentityServerConstants.LocalApi.PolicyName. Mentions that clients must include 'IdentityServerApi' in their allowed scopes. Warns that this only works when co-hosted with IdentityServer.
assertions: Calls AddLocalApiAuthentication() to set up local API authentication
Uses IdentityServerConstants.LocalApi.PolicyName to protect endpoints
Mentions that clients must include 'IdentityServerApi' in their AllowedScopes
Warns that local API authentication only works when the API is co-hosted with IdentityServer, not for separate API hosts

evals.prompt: I'm getting 401 Unauthorized when my API validates tokens but I can't figure out why. The JWT looks valid. What are common pitfalls with JWT bearer configuration in APIs protected by IdentityServer?
evals.expected_output: A troubleshooting guide covering audience mismatch between JwtBearerOptions.Audience and ApiResource name, missing ValidTypes for at+jwt, scope claim format inconsistency, and middleware ordering issues.
assertions: Mentions audience mismatch as a common cause — Audience in JwtBearerOptions must match the ApiResource name in IdentityServer
Mentions the importance of ValidTypes = ['at+jwt'] and warns about JWT confusion attacks without it
Mentions scope claim format inconsistency — space-delimited vs array — as a cause of silent authorization failures
Mentions checking the middleware pipeline ordering (UseAuthentication before UseAuthorization)
